actionable_predicate: Long-horizon, identity-bearing work (multi-year migrations, regulated platforms, foundational services) requires staffing on trained equanimity (the qualifier kalpate names) plus verified care; absence of the equanimity is a disqualifier, not a deficiency to coach later.
example_decision: Tech-lead selection for a five-year ledger migration. Three internal candidates. Apply the verse: rule out Candidate A (visibly fluctuating posture, destabilises on-call channel) on the equanimity check. Rule in Candidate B (consistent posture, demonstrated track record across long-horizon projects) on the equanimity check, then verify care (do they engage with users, with team?) before final selection. Candidate C is excellent but untested through long-horizon — defer until tested. The verse changes the staffing decision.
objection: none — the predicate yields a falsifiable, observable check (posture under repeated incidents, demonstrated track record, verification of care). Two PMs could disagree about whether a candidate has 'enough' equanimity, but the disagreement is over evidence, which is the productive kind.
